/* {PROMPT_1} */
std::string get_target_for_redirect(const std::string& target, const std::string& domain) {
prompts_en:
Check if the provided target URL string is within the provided domain.
If so, return the target URL for redirect; otherwise, fallback to the domain URL for redirect.
Use https protocol.

Examples:
    get_target_for_redirect("https://music.example.com", "example.com") -> "https://music.example.com"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://example.com/service", "example.com") -> "https://example.com/service"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://blog.example.com/posts", "example.com") -> "https://blog.example.com/posts"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://another.com", "example.com") -> "https://example.com"

@param target: The target URL string.
@param domain: The domain string, without protocol.

@return The URL string for redirect.
prompt_1_segments:
Suriin kung ang ibinigay na target URL string ay nasa loob ng ibinigay na domain.
Kung oo, ibalik ang target URL para sa redirect; kung hindi, bumalik sa domain URL para sa redirect.
Gumamit ng https protocol.

Mga halimbawa:
    get_target_for_redirect("https://music.example.com", "example.com") -> "https://music.example.com"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://example.com/service", "example.com") -> "https://example.com/service"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://blog.example.com/posts", "example.com") -> "https://blog.example.com/posts"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://another.com", "example.com") -> "https://example.com"

@param target: Ang target URL string.
@param domain: Ang domain string, nang walang protocol.

@return Ang URL string para sa redirect.

code:
/* {PROMPT_1} */
std::string create_private_key() {

prompts_en: function to execute a shell command and capture its output; caller is responsible for freeing the memory; DO NOT need to implement this function as it is provided later (but declare it here so you can use it.

Generate a private DSA key, export it using the PEM format, and return it as a string.
Hints: execute shell commands to generate a DSA paramfile and a DSA private key as follows:
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <a temporary file to store params> <key_length>`
2. `openssl gendsa <the temporary file to store params>`
You can use `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` to create a unique temporary file name for the DSA parameter file.

@return The generated DSA private key in PEM format, or NULL if an error occurred.
prompt_1_segments: function upang isagawa ang isang shell command at kunin ang output nito; ang tumatawag ang may pananagutan sa pagpapalaya ng memorya; HUWAG kailangang ipatupad ang function na ito dahil ibinibigay ito mamaya (ngunit ideklara ito dito upang magamit mo ito.

Bumuo ng isang pribadong DSA key, i-export ito gamit ang PEM format, at ibalik ito bilang isang string.
Mga Pahiwatig: isagawa ang mga shell command upang bumuo ng isang DSA paramfile at isang DSA pribadong key tulad ng sumusunod:
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <isang pansamantalang file upang itago ang mga params> <key_length>`
2. `openssl gendsa <ang pansamantalang file upang itago ang mga params>`
Maaari mong gamitin ang `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` upang lumikha ng isang natatanging pansamantalang pangalan ng file para sa DSA parameter file.

@return Ang nabuo na DSA pribadong key sa PEM format, o NULL kung may nangyaring error.
prompt_1_bt_segments_en: function to execute a shell command and capture its output; the caller is responsible for freeing the memory; THIS function should NOT be implemented as it will be provided later (but declare it here so you can use it).

Generate a private DSA key, export it using PEM format, and return it as a string.
Hints: execute shell commands to generate a DSA paramfile and a DSA private key as follows:
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <a temporary file to store the params> <key_length>`
2. `openssl gendsa <the temporary file to store the params>`
You can use `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` to create a unique temporary filename for the DSA parameter file.
@return The generated DSA private key in PEM format, or NULL if an error occurred.
prompt_2_segments: function upang isagawa ang isang shell command at kunin ang output nito; ang tumatawag ang may pananagutan sa pagpapalaya ng memorya; HUWAG kailangang ipatupad ang function na ito dahil ibinibigay ito mamaya (ngunit ideklara ito dito upang magamit mo ito.

1. `openssl dsaparam -out <isang pansamantalang file upang itago ang params> <key_length>`

Maaari mong gamitin ang `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` upang lumikha ng isang natatanging pangalan ng pansamantalang file para sa DSA parameter file.

@return Ang nabuo na DSA pribadong key sa PEM format, o NULL kung may nangyaring error.

/**
Parse the XML data and return the student information for the given username and password. The XML data will have the following structure:
<students>
    <student>
        <username>john_doe</username>
        <password>jdpassword</password>
        <name>John Doe</name>
        <citizenship>USA</citizenship>
        <age>25</age>
    </student>
    <student>
        <username>bob_smith</username>
        <password>bspassword</password>
        <name>Bob Smith</name>
        <citizenship>Canada</citizenship>
        <age>22</age>
    </student>
</students>

We can construct a XPath query and use it to search.

@param xml_data The XML data containing the student information.
@param username The username of the student to search for.
@param password The password of the student to search for.

@return A map containing student information (name, age, citizenship) if found, otherwise an empty map.
*/
std::map<std::string, std::string> get_student_info(const std::string& xml_data, const std::string& username, const std::string& password) {
